correction.method: Browser tab throttle correction. Each tick->tick gap > 2.5s was treated as a throttled wall-clock interval. Productive_dt within each gap was inferred from mass_after delta divided by mps_at_start, then the excess (raw_gap - productive_dt) was subtracted from all subsequent event timestamps so the timeline reflects productive (un-throttled) wall-clock only. Aggregate fields in the end event (cohesion_hit_ms, total_time_ms, completionist_extension_ms) and tier_snapshots t_ms fields were correspondingly remapped.
correction.note: Pause/resume events in the source already share t_ms (pause/resume durations excluded from t_ms by the logger), so no additional pause correction was needed beyond throttle handling.

flags: N=1 T3 playtest. Single data point; calibration band loose until more T3 data lands.
Effective CPM during the productive timeline averaged ~60 cpm (autoclicker setting).

The throttled wall-clock window between ~37:53 and ~46:21 of the source log (player tabbed away) totaled 8.93 min and was removed. Bot effectively paused during this window: clicks froze, mass advanced ~1.2s of productive time per 60s wake.
A separate 24.55s throttle gap near the very end was also removed.
Cohesion (Threshold) timing is unaffected by the correction because the throttle began after cohesion was reached.
